unit: Ὅ6 Unit 6: Systems of Linear Equations and Inequalities
topic: Systems of Linear Inequalities by Graphing

questions.question: Graph the system of inequalities: $x + y geq 2$ and $x - y leq 1$. What is the shape of the solution region?
A book costs $$15$ and a pen costs $$5$. Graph the system of inequalities representing the total cost of buying $x$ books and $y$ pens, given that the total budget is $$50$.
A company produces two types of products: $x$ and $y$. The profit from $x$ is $$20$ per unit and from $y$ is $$15$ per unit. The graph of the system of inequalities representing the total profit is

Tom has $$25$ to spend on video games and snacks. Video games cost $$10$ each and snacks cost $$5$ each. Graph the system of inequalities representing the total cost of buying $x$ video games and $y$ snacks.
A bakery sells two types of cakes: $x$ and $y$. The system of inequalities representing the total number of cakes sold is $x + y leq 20$ and $x - y geq 5$. What is the minimum number of cakes sold?

A student has 12 hours to study for two exams. The system of inequalities representing the time spent studying for each exam is $x + y leq 12$ and $x - y geq 2$. What is the maximum time spent studying for the first exam?

A store sells two types of shirts: $x$ and $y$. The profit from $x$ is $$10$ per unit and from $y$ is $$8$ per unit. The system of inequalities representing the total profit is $10x + 8y geq 50$ and $x - y leq 5$. What is the minimum profit?

A company produces two types of products: $x$ and $y$. The cost of producing $x$ is $$5$ per unit and the cost of producing $y$ is $$3$ per unit. The system of inequalities representing the total cost is $5x + 3y leq 50$ and $x + y geq 10$. What is the minimum cost?
Graph the system of inequalities: $2x + y geq 4$ and $x - 2y leq 3$. Identify the overlapping solution region and interpret the feasible region.

A student has 15 hours to study for three exams. The system of inequalities representing the time spent studying for each exam is $x + y + z leq 15$ and $x - y geq 2$ and $y - z leq 3$. Graph the system and identify the minimum time spent studying for the first exam.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of graphing multiple inequalities on the same coordinate plane.
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of identifying overlapping solution regions and interpreting feasible regions.
Tip 3: Encourage students to use real-life scenarios to understand the application of systems of linear inequalities.
